0:{"rsc":["$","$1","c",{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"page-enter","children":[["$","$L2",null,{}],["$","article",null,{"className":"article-detail","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"container-tight","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog","className":"back-link","children":"← Back to all essays"}],["$","header",null,{"className":"article-header","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"article-header-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"children":"02"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Design"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"March 12, 2026"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["8 min"," read"]}]]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"Design Systems That Survive Their First Year"}],["$","p",null,{"style":{"fontSize":19,"color":"var(--ink-soft)","lineHeight":1.5,"margin":0,"maxWidth":600},"children":"Most design systems die from neglect, not bad design. What separates the ones that last from the ones that become a graveyard of deprecated components."}]]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container-tight","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"img-slot hero-image","data-label":"Design","aria-hidden":true}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container-tight","children":["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","p","0",{"className":"lede","children":"A design system is easy to start and hard to keep. The launch is a party; year two is the test. The systems that survive share a few unglamorous traits."}],["$","h2","1",{"children":"Components are a contract, not a suggestion"}],["$","p","2",{"children":"If a button can be overridden into anything, it isn't a button — it's a styled div with extra steps. Constrain the API to the decisions that actually matter, and make the rest impossible."}],["$","h2","3",{"children":"Tokens before components"}],["$","p","4",{"children":"Ship color, spacing, and type scales first. They're cheap to adopt incrementally and they make every later component consistent for free."}],["$","h2","5",{"children":"Adoption is a product problem"}],["$","ul","6",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"children":"Migrate one real screen yourself before asking anyone else to."}],["$","li","1",{"children":"Treat the docs site like a product with its own users."}],["$","li","2",{"children":"Measure adoption like a funnel — what's installed, what's imported, what's actually rendered."}]]}],["$","div","7",{"className":"pull","children":["“","A component nobody can find is a component nobody will use.","”"]}],["$","h2","8",{"children":"Plan for deprecation on day one"}],["$","p","9",{"children":"You will get things wrong. The question is whether removing a mistake costs an afternoon or a quarter. Version your components, log usage, and make the deprecation path as well-lit as the adoption one."}]]}]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"container","style":{"marginTop":96,"paddingTop":48,"borderTop":"1px solid var(--rule-soft)"},"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"eyebrow","style":{"marginBottom":16},"children":"Read next"}],["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/blog/the-edge-is-just-a-computer","className":"article-row","children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"article-num","children":"03"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","div",null,{"className":"article-title","children":"The Edge Is Just a Computer (a Smaller One)"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-excerpt","children":"Edge functions feel magical until they don't. A practical mental model for what actually changes when your code runs close to the user."}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-meta","children":[["$","div",null,{"children":"January 30, 2026"}],["$","div",null,{"style":{"marginTop":4},"children":"Web"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"article-read","children":["read",["$","br",null,{}],"5 min"]}]]}]]}]]}]]}],[["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/myblog/_next/static/chunks/0ij507p0cynli.js","async":true}]],["$","$L4",null,{"children":["$","$5",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@6"}]}]]}],"isPartial":false,"staleTime":300,"varyParams":null,"buildId":"cq9Q9J7UkxT6Kp5YPUbwV"}
